entry.title: RECAP Public Archive: 0 Cases Contributed. Help Us Change That.
entry.summary: Every case we verify requires accessing PACER documents behind a paywall. The RECAP browser extension automatically contributes those documents to the free public archive at CourtListener. There are 37.9 million bankruptcy cases in the federal system behind PACER's paywall, with more added daily. We have contributed 0 so far. Our goal: make every case we touch freely accessible. Every PACER document we access with RECAP installed becomes permanently free for the next researcher, attorney, journalist, or debtor. Support this effort at https://1328f.org/support/

entry.title: 1328f.org Launches: 9 Reports, 34 Pages, Bilingual, Open Source
entry.summary: 1328f.org is live with 9 empirical reports covering BAPCPA, Chapter 13 dismissal rates, bankruptcy mill definitions, attorney performance methodology, Section 109(g), Section 727(a)(8), Section 1328(f) screening, prior-filer discharge rates, and a PACER consumer guide. All content available in English and Spanish. Research library with 12 academic citations. Donations accepted via Ko-fi.

entry.title: Rules Committee Submission Accepted
entry.summary: Empirical data submitted to the Administrative Office of the U.S. Courts in support of Suggestion 26-BK-3, the Connelly/Kahn proposed amendment to Federal Rule of Bankruptcy Procedure 4004. The submission includes data from 4.9 million cases across 94 districts, an open-source screening tool, and a public GitHub repository. The AO Rules Committee Counsel requested the submission be forwarded for posting as a related suggestion on uscourts.gov.

entry.title: Section 1328(f) Screening Complete: 264 Verified Violations in Multi-District Sample
entry.summary: Completed de-staling of all flagged cases across 94 federal bankruptcy districts. In a verified 7-district sample, 264 unique cases where debtors received Chapter 13 discharges despite falling within the Section 1328(f) statutory bar window. Filing-date-to-filing-date methodology per In re Blendheim. 6 attorneys account for 73% of violations.

entry.title: National District Map Launched
entry.summary: Interactive map of all 94 federal bankruptcy districts now live at 1328f.com. Includes screening status, violation counts, prior-filer rates, and sortable district table. Data derived from FJC Integrated Database (Ch.13 cases FY2008-2024).
